name: Frappe Assistant Core MCP
what_it_does: Frappe Assistant Core MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Frappe Framework and ERPNext — querying DocTypes and documents, executing Frappe methods, managing ERPNext business data, and integrating Frappe's open-source ERP capabilities into agent-driven business process automation workflows.
use_cases: Querying ERPNext business documents from business process agents
Managing Frappe DocTypes and records from data management agents
Automating ERPNext workflows from business automation agents
Generating business reports from ERP data from reporting agents
Integrating ERPNext with AI-driven business intelligence workflows
Building Frappe app features with AI assistance from development agents
not_for: Non-Frappe ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics require different MCPs)
High-volume transactions where MCP latency is unacceptable
best_when: A business using Frappe Framework or ERPNext needs AI agents to query and manage business data — open-source ERP with AI-powered automation.
avoid_when: You're using SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, or other ERP systems — Frappe-specific tools only work with Frappe/ERPNext.

auth.notes: Frappe API key/secret required or username/password. Configured in Frappe user settings. Works with self-hosted or Frappe Cloud instances.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Frappe Framework and ERPNext are free open source (self-hosted)
pricing.paid_tiers: Frappe Cloud hosting: from $10/month; ERPNext support plans
pricing.notes: Frappe/ERPNext are free and open source. Self-hosting is free; Frappe Cloud has paid tiers. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Frappe REST API limits — self-hosted may have no limits

known_agent_gotchas: Frappe DocType schema knowledge required — agents need to understand data model
API key must have appropriate DocType permissions — insufficient permissions cause silent failures
Self-hosted instances have different URLs — configure endpoint correctly
Community MCP from buildswithpaul — not official Frappe tooling
Frappe versions (v13, v14, v15) have API differences — verify compatibility
Business data in ERP — handle with appropriate access control and governance
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for cloud. API key auth — use minimal permissions. Community MCP. ERP data is business-critical — apply appropriate access controls and audit logging.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. Business-critical ERP data. Community MCP. Apply minimum-privilege access control.
